steps: The first 25 people each cost \$22, so the base charge is $25 \times 22 = 550$ dollars.
For a group of $n$ people ($n \geq 25$), the additional people number $n - 25$, each costing \$14. Additional charge $= 14(n-25) = 14n - 350$.
The Desmos row graphs this final function for $n \geq 25$ so you can check that the model starts at $f(25)=550$ and increases by $14$ for each additional person.
explanation:
• Choice A — '$f(n)=14n+200$': Derived directly above: $550 + 14(n-25) = 14n + 200$. ✓
  • Choice B — '$f(n)=14n+22$': The constant 22 makes no sense — it ignores the 550 base and does not subtract the already-counted 25 people. ✗
  • Choice C — '$f(n)=14n+550$': This would give $550 + 14n$ without subtracting the $14 \times 25 = 350$ for the first 25 people who should be charged at \$22 not \$14, so it double-counts. ✗
  • Choice D — '$f(n)=36n-350$': A flat rate of \$36/person does not match the two-tier pricing described; at $n=25$, this gives $36(25)-350 = 550$ coincidentally, but at $n=26$ it gives 586 while the correct answer gives 564. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
